request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Marijuana rescheduled by December 31, 2026?
Resolution criteria: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if marijuana is rescheduled down from Schedule I
to any other level (or is unscheduled completely) by the Drug Enforcement Agency of
the United States by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will
resolve to 'No'.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. government,
however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Note: In April 2026, FDA-approved marijuana products and state-licensed medical
cannabis were moved to Schedule III under President Trump's executive order. This
market specifically tracks whether recreational marijuana (currently Schedule I) is
rescheduled. If the DEA reschedules only specific cannabinoids or derivatives without
reclassifying marijuana as a whole, that does not qualify.

A DEA administrative hearing on full rescheduling is scheduled to begin June 29, 2026.

Conditions:
  A: DEA lowers schedule for marijuana — By Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a final DEA action reclassifies marijuana (the whole plant/substance, i.e., recreational marijuana) from Schedule I to II–V. Not satisfied by rescheduling only cannabinoids/derivatives.
  B: DEA unschedules marijuana — By Dec 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a final DEA action removes marijuana entirely from the Controlled Substances Act schedules (unscheduled).
Settlement entities: ['Drug Enforcement Administration', 'Department of Health and Human Services', 'Department of Justice', 'Food and Drug Administration', 'White House Office of National Drug Control Policy']

Record title: Attorney General Bonta Opposes Trump Administration’s Proposed Expansion of Catastrophic Health Insurance Plans
Record date: 2026-03-13
Record regulator: Office of the Attorney General of California
Record topic: Office of the Attorney General, California Department of Justice
Record entities: ['Andrea Joy Campbell', 'California', 'Dr. Mehmet Oz', 'Jennifer Davenport', 'Office of the Attorney General of Arizona', 'Office of the Attorney General of California', 'Office of the Attorney General of Colorado', 'Office of the Attorney General of Delaware', 'Office of the Attorney General of Hawai’i', 'Office of the Attorney General of Illinois', 'Office of the Attorney General of Maine', 'Office of the Attorney General of Maryland', 'Office of the Attorney General of Massachusetts', 'Office of the Attorney General of Michigan', 'Office of the Attorney General of Minnesota', 'Office of the Attorney General of Nevada', 'Office of the Attorney General of New Jersey', 'Office of the Attorney General of New Mexico', 'Office of the Attorney General of New York', 'Office of the Attorney General of Oregon', 'Office of the Attorney General of Rhode Island', 'Office of the Attorney General of Washington', 'Office of the Attorney General of Wisconsin', 'Rob Bonta']

response.one_line_why: Off-topic: CA Attorney General opposing health-insurance changes; unrelated to federal DEA marijuana rescheduling, so no effect.
